title: Quiz: Entanglement-Breaking Channels
description: Test your understanding of entanglement-breaking channels

questions.question: An entanglement-breaking channel is characterized by:

options.text: Making any bipartite state separable when applied to one part

questions.explanation: An EB channel makes any bipartite state separable when applied to one subsystem, destroying all entanglement.

questions.question: The Holevo form of an EB channel is:
questions.explanation: The Holevo form shows EB channels as measure-and-prepare: measure with POVM {M_k}, prepare state sigma_k based on outcome.

questions.question: Entanglement-breaking channels always have zero quantum capacity.
questions.explanation: True. Since EB channels destroy all entanglement, they cannot transmit quantum information, giving Q = 0.

questions.question: The qubit depolarizing channel is EB when:
questions.explanation: For d=2 (qubits), the threshold is p >= d/(d+1) = 2/3.

questions.question: An EB channel has Kraus operators that are:
questions.explanation: EB channels can always be written with rank-1 Kraus operators: K_k = |psi_k><phi_k|.

questions.question: The Choi matrix of an EB channel is:
questions.explanation: The Choi matrix of an EB channel is always separable, which provides an alternative characterization of EB channels.

questions.question: All entanglement-breaking channels are anti-degradable.
questions.explanation: True. EB channels are anti-degradable because the environment (performing the measurement) captures all the quantum information.

questions.question: If N is EB and M is any channel, then N o M is:

options.text: EB only if M is unitary
EB only if M is also EB

questions.explanation: Composing any channel followed by an EB channel gives an EB channel - once entanglement is broken, it cannot be restored.
